findings.title: Transaction reads PR doc even for duplicate findings
findings.description: In `recordFinding`, the PR doc is read via `tx.get(prRef)` inside the transaction even when the finding already exists (`findingSnap.exists` → early return). This is a wasted read for idempotent retries.

findings.title: Backfill uses review.id (body) not reviewDoc.id (key)
findings.description: The main loop extracts `reviewId` from `review.id` (the document body field), but `recomputeParentPr` correctly uses `reviewDoc.id` (the Firestore document key). Legacy review docs written by older code paths may not populate the `id` field in the body, causing the script to skip valid reviews with `[skip] ... missing orgId/prId/id field`. Using `reviewDoc.id` would match the pattern used later in the same script.

findings.title: Backfill hardcodes collection paths, not shared helpers
findings.description: The backfill script duplicates Firestore collection names ('organizations', 'pullRequests', 'reviews', 'findings') as inline strings instead of importing from the shared path helpers in pull-request-manager.ts. If a collection name ever changes, the backfill would silently diverge from canonical storage paths.

findings.title: listPRs lacks where('orgId') unlike listPullRequests
findings.description: `listPullRequests` adds a defense-in-depth `.where('orgId', '==', orgId)` to match orgId-leading indexes. `listPRs` relies solely on path scoping without the explicit clause. If `listPRs` were called with a state filter, the query would need an index that doesn't exist in `firestore.indexes.json`.

findings.title: Redundant orgId filter could silently exclude migrated docs
findings.description: `listPullRequests` queries `prCollection(orgId)` (already scoped to `organizations/{orgId}/pullRequests`) and adds `.where('orgId', '==', orgId)`. If any document has a missing or mismatched `orgId` field (e.g., created during migration), it will be silently excluded from results.

findings.title: Dry-run adds to touchedPrs for already-copied reviews
findings.description: When `alreadyAtTarget` is true (review already copied in a prior run), the code still adds the key to `touchedPrs`, triggering `recomputeParentPr` even in dry-run mode. This produces misleading output suggesting work would be done when it wouldn't.

findings.title: hasFindingsMin: optional (params) vs nullable (PullRequest)
findings.description: `CodeReviewPullsParams.hasFindingsMin` is `CodeReviewSeverity | undefined` (optional). `PullRequest.hasFindingsMin` is `CodeReviewSeverity | null` (required, nullable). The mixed optional/nullable pattern could confuse contributors.

findings.title: getPullRequest duplicates getPR with identical logic
findings.description: `getPullRequest` and `getPR` have identical signatures and implementations. Having two names for the same operation forces future maintainers to guess which one to call.

findings.title: updatePRStats appears unused after refactor
findings.description: `updatePRStats` overwrites the entire stats sub-document. After this PR, stats fields are managed incrementally inside `completeReview` (which uses dot-notation like `'stats.reviewCount'`). No current consumer calls `updatePRStats` — the incremental approach supersedes it.

findings.title: completeReview sets latestReviewId without timestamp check
findings.description: In `completeReview`, `latestReviewId` is always set to the current `reviewId` without comparing completion timestamps. The backfill script's `recomputeParentPr` correctly compares `completedAt` to find the true latest, but the live code path does not.

findings.title: getPR/getPRByNumber/listPRs are dead code
findings.description: The PR introduces `getPullRequest` and `listPullRequests` which duplicate the older `getPR`, `getPRByNumber`, and `listPRs` methods. All consumers now use the new methods exclusively, making the old ones unreachable dead code.
